summary: Round-1 major finding T1-M1 is fully resolved. ForeignKeysOnOpenCallback was widened from private to internal val, all three androidTest @Before blocks now explicitly attach it to their inMemoryDatabaseBuilder, and a new foreignKeysPragmaIsOn test reads PRAGMA foreign_keys directly and asserts it equals 1 — together this closes the bypass path the round-1 report identified. The new tests (insertAndGetByIdPreservesProjectIdValue, nullableFieldsRoundTrip, observeByChatFiltersByChatId) add meaningful coverage for Decision 1's nullable project_id, MessageEntity nullable fields, and the observeByChat WHERE-clause. The rewritten observeAllEmitsOnInsert uses a live MutableSharedFlow collector so the test would now fail if Room invalidation regressed. No new blocking or major issues introduced. One small nit on a 50 ms delay() inside observeAllEmitsOnInsert; not worth a dedicated fix.

findings.issue: observeAllEmitsOnInsert relies on a delay(50L) before the triggering insert to let the collector 'resubscribe for the next emission'. In practice the test is protected by withTimeout(5_000L) on the post-insert await, and MutableSharedFlow was declared with extraBufferCapacity = 16 so a missed emission would still be buffered — which means the sleep is load-bearing only as a race softener against the launch(Dispatchers.IO) scheduling latency, not for correctness.
findings.impact: None today — the test passes deterministically on the current runner. Magic sleep durations tend to bite when CI is slow or when the test is copied as a template for future DAO tests.
findings.recommendation: Optional polish: replace the delay(50L) + .first { it.isNotEmpty() } pattern with a CompletableDeferred-based latch, or drop the sleep entirely and rely on the buffered SharedFlow + withTimeout to absorb scheduling jitter. Deferrable; keep the fix small if tackled.

evidence: app/src/androidTest/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/data/SanctumDatabaseTest.kt:42-51 — new foreignKeysPragmaIsOn test asserts PRAGMA foreign_keys == 1 by querying sqlite directly; independent belt-and-braces proof the callback ran. foreignKeyEnforcementEnabled and onDeleteCascadeRemovesMessages now run against the same production callback object, so a regression removing the callback wiring would be caught by the PRAGMA assertion at minimum.

resolved_from_round_1.note: Visibility widened to internal as suggested (the minimal fix variant). Converting the anonymous Callback subclass to a Kotlin 'object ForeignKeysOnOpenCallback : Callback()' was cosmetic; left as-is. No action needed.
Kotlin/kotlin-reflect version drift is unrelated to Task 1; correctly not touched by the round-1 fix. Track as tech debt for a catalog-hygiene pass.
Index auto-naming vs tech-spec SQL sketch names — stylistic; schema JSON is authoritative. No action.
DATABASE_NAME constant unused in this commit — Task 6 (SanctumApplication/AppModule) is the expected consumer. No action for Task 1.
Single-letter test fixture labels — readability nit, correctly untouched.

observeAllEmitsOnInsert was rewritten (see T1-M1 evidence) and is now genuinely distinct from observeAllEmitsSortedByLastMessageDesc — it proves re-emission on insert via a live collector, not just repeated .first() calls. The overlap concern from round 1 is gone.
readTableNames() and two 'absent-table' tests — cosmetic. No action.

new_code_assessment.SanctumDatabase.kt_visibility_change: Clean. 'internal val' is the minimal, idiomatic widening; matches the round-1 recommendation. No test-only API leaked to main module consumers beyond Room's Callback contract.
new_code_assessment.foreignKeysPragmaIsOn_test: Clean. Uses .use { cursor } for lifecycle, asserts with a clear failure message, queries PRAGMA directly. Independent of messageDao insert behaviour — catches a regression where the callback is registered but the PRAGMA is set on the wrong connection.
new_code_assessment.insertAndGetByIdPreservesProjectIdValue: Clean. Minimal fixture (projectId = 42L), round-trips through insert + getById, guards Decision 1 (nullable project_id without FK).
new_code_assessment.nullableFieldsRoundTrip: Clean. Covers thinkingText, imagePath, audioPath, tokenCount in one insert/read — previously uncovered nullable columns on MessageEntity.
new_code_assessment.observeByChatFiltersByChatId: Clean. Two-chat fixture proves the WHERE chat_id = :chatId clause actually filters; a missing WHERE would previously have been invisible.
new_code_assessment.observeAllEmitsOnInsert_rewrite: Good in intent, minor style nit in execution (see T1-R2-NT1). The MutableSharedFlow(replay = 0, extraBufferCapacity = 16) + withTimeout pattern is correct; the delay(50L) is not strictly needed and is the one piece of magic in an otherwise tight test.

acceptance_criteria_check.decision_1_compliance: PASS + strengthened — insertAndGetByIdPreservesProjectIdValue now explicitly exercises a non-null project_id value.
acceptance_criteria_check.scope_discipline_no_feature_creep: PASS — round-1 fix did not introduce repository, module, or coordinator code; strictly test + visibility adjustment.
acceptance_criteria_check.fk_enforcement_verified_by_test: PASS — production ForeignKeysOnOpenCallback is now the exact object registered on the test builders; foreignKeysPragmaIsOn is an independent proof that PRAGMA foreign_keys resolves to 1 on the test connection; foreignKeyEnforcementEnabled and onDeleteCascadeRemovesMessages therefore validate the production contract, not runtime-default coincidence.

verdict_rationale: T1-M1 is resolved with a tight, targeted fix: minimum-possible visibility change on the main module, three test @Before updates, and one new PRAGMA-reading test that provides an independent regression guard. The supplementary new tests (insertAndGetByIdPreservesProjectIdValue, nullableFieldsRoundTrip, observeByChatFiltersByChatId, rewritten observeAllEmitsOnInsert) add real coverage without scope creep. Remaining round-1 items were correctly classified as deferrable minors/nits and did not need to be picked up. Only a single nit on a 50 ms delay() in one test — not worth blocking on. Task 1 is ready to finalize.
